ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for butterfly fritter; mapped to the generic fritter class as the closest defensible ancestor. Butterfly fritter is a shaped deep-fried dough product studded with sesame seeds.
ontology.foodex2_description: Doughnuts and similar sweet fried dough products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKW covers sweet fried dough products; butterfly fritter is a sesame-studded deep-fried dough snack that fits this category as the closest defensible code. No exact butterfly fritter code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to this specific snack food; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one butterfly fritter piece weighing approximately 40 g, providing around 146 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Butterfly Fritter — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Butterfly Fritter (CR ID F11090004). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical entry at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for similar deep-fried wheat dough products (USDA SR Legacy #18033, Doughnuts, cake-type, plain) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for butterfly fritter in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 76 (high) based on the closest analogous food: deep-fried wheat dough products (doughnuts, cake-type) which have a reported GI of approximately 76 per Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Butterfly fritter shares the same refined wheat flour base, deep-frying method, and added sugar profile. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × available carbohydrate per serving (g) / 100 = 76 × 49.9 × 0.40 / 100 = 15.1 (40 g serving, 19.96 g available carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (13.0 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (1.9 g/100 g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 139 mg/100 g is moderate but the sugar-fibre imbalance independently elevates cardiovascular risk.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid butterfly fritter due to its high sugar content (13.0 g/100 g) and low fibre (1.9 g/100 g), which promotes insulin-driven sodium retention and vascular inflammation from oxidised frying fats.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~76), high refined carbohydrate (49.9 g/100 g), high sugar (13.0 g/100 g), and low dietary fibre (1.9 g/100 g) cause rapid postprandial glucose spikes; deep-frying adds trans fat that worsens insulin resistance.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid butterfly fritter entirely; its high-GI refined flour base, 13.0 g/100 g sugar, and estimated trans fat from deep-frying combine to cause rapid glucose excursions and worsen insulin resistance.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying generates trans fat (~0.3 g/100 g) and advanced glycation end products (AGEs); saturated fat (~3.2 g/100 g) and high sugar (13.0 g/100 g) further elevate LDL and triglyceride risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid butterfly fritter due to its trans fat content from deep-frying, estimated saturated fat of 3.2 g/100 g, and high sugar load, all of which adversely affect lipid profiles and endothelial function.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates AGEs that directly worsen joint inflammation; trans fat (~0.3 g/100 g) is strongly pro-inflammatory; sugar at 13.0 g/100 g is also pro-inflammatory.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid butterfly fritter entirely; deep-frying produces AGEs and trans fat that directly amplify joint inflammation and disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Butterfly fritter is a traditional Singaporean deep-fried snack made from a sweetened wheat flour dough that is shaped into a butterfly form and studded with sesame seeds before frying. The dough is typically prepared from refined wheat flour, sugar, eggs, and water, then deep-fried in vegetable oil until golden and crisp. The result is a light, crunchy exterior with a slightly chewy interior, carrying a mild sweetness and a nutty sesame aroma. It is commonly sold at traditional kopitiams, pasar malam (night markets), and local snack stalls across Singapore and Malaysia. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 364 kcal, 6.7 g protein, 15.3 g fat, 49.9 g carbohydrate, 13.0 g sugar, 1.9 g dietary fibre, and 139 mg sodium. The high refined carbohydrate content, elevated sugar, and deep-frying method give it a high glycemic index (estimated ~76), making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. Trans fat generated during deep-frying and advanced glycation end products (AGEs) further elevate inflammatory and cardiovascular risk. The sesame seeds contribute small amounts of calcium (33 mg/100 g) and iron (1.2 mg/100 g). This food is plant-based and halal-suitable but contains gluten and sesame allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a butterfly fritter?
Is butterfly fritter high in sugar?
What are the ingredients in butterfly fritter?
Is butterfly fritter suitable for hypertension?
Where can I buy butterfly fritter in Singapore?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F11090004) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy closest analogue (cake-type doughnut). GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (deep-fried wheat dough). All mandatory multilingual names provided for Singaporean cuisine category.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for butterfly fritter, and no supplementary approved source provides these values for this specific traditional preparation. Trans_fat_g is set to 0.3 g/100 g as an estimate based on typical deep-frying oil contribution for similar products; this is not directly reported by HPB but is consistent with the input record's clinical assessment and USDA data for comparable fried dough products. Saturated_fat_g of 3.2 g/100 g is estimated from USDA SR Legacy #18033 (Doughnuts, cake-type, plain) as the closest approved-source analogue, as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate based on deep-fried wheat dough products from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008. No core macro (protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) is zero; all are non-zero and sourced from HPB.
